crossings.name: Mezyad / Buraimi
crossings.notes: PRIMARY EVAC ROUTE. ~2hr from Dubai. Multiple crossing points. Security delays. E66 highway intact. Best option.

crossings.name: Al Wajajah (Hatta)
crossings.notes: Alt route via Hatta mountains. ~5-6hr to Muscat. Scenic/mountainous — slower.

crossings.name: Khatmat Malaha
crossings.notes: Eastern coastal route. Dibba crossing. Good alternative to Mezyad.

crossings.name: Al Rawdah
crossings.notes: Opened Feb 26. Additional capacity. Less congested than Mezyad currently.

crossings.name: Al Ghuwaifat / Silaa
crossings.notes: NOT RECOMMENDED. ~1,100km / 10-12hr to Riyadh. Transits 250km Gulf coast near active energy targets. Riyadh under direct attack. Use only if Oman route unavailable.

crossings.name: Al Wajba (Haima area)
crossings.notes: Interior desert route. Low traffic. Operational.

crossings.name: Shaybah Desert Crossing
crossings.notes: Remote crossing. Status unconfirmed. Not recommended without verification.

crossings.name: Al Salmi / Nuwaiseeb
crossings.notes: Key US military logistics corridor. Security heightened. 6 US service members KIA in Kuwait. Monitor closely.

crossings.name: Al Hadithah / Mudawwarah
crossings.notes: Western-friendly corridor. Viable alternative evacuation route via Jordan → Amman → fly out.

crossings.name: Al Wadiah / Haradh
crossings.notes: ACTIVE CONFLICT ZONE. Houthi proximity. DO NOT USE.

crossings.name: Sharurah (Najran)
crossings.notes: Military zone. Conflict active. DO NOT USE.

crossings.name: Arar / Turaif
crossings.notes: Iraqi Shia militia activity in region. Border restricted. Monitor.
